faqs.q: Is high-functioning anxiety a real diagnosis?
faqs.a: No. High-functioning anxiety is a popular label, not a formal diagnosis, and it is not in the DSM-5-TR. It describes feeling persistent anxiety inside while appearing capable outside, and it often overlaps with generalized anxiety disorder.

faqs.q: What does high-functioning anxiety feel like?
faqs.a: People often describe a motor that never idles, with constant worry, restlessness, replaying conversations, and trouble relaxing, all while still meeting their responsibilities. The strain often shows up quietly in sleep, tension, irritability, or burnout.

Yes. Anxiety responds well to treatment even when someone is still functioning well. Therapy approaches like CBT and, when appropriate, medication can help. A clinician can assess whether it meets the threshold for an anxiety disorder.
